description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline using ubuntu:17.10 as the default Docker image, with a global before_script that prints job start information, echoes project details, and lists the repository, and a cache keyed "2017-08-07d" that stores $VM_DIR, $MIN_DIR and $PHARO_CACHE_DIR. Define stages setup, build, test, and deploy and set global variables AWS_DEFAULT_OUTPUT=json, AWS_DEFAULT_REGION=eu-west-1, LAMBDA_NAME=$CI_PROJECT_NAME, MIN_DIR=pharo-min, PHARO_CACHE_DIR=pharo-cache, VM_DIR=pharo-vm. In the setup stage add a setup job that runs conditional apt‑get update/install curl unzip, downloads a Pharo 64‑bit VM and image via curl, downloads a minimal Pharo image zip from Dropbox, extracts it, creates a build directory, copies the VM files and, if $USE_MIN_IMAGE is true, copies the minimal image, then archives the build directory as an artifact and sets GIT_STRATEGY=none. In the build stage add a generate job that echoes installation messages, exports DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive, runs apt‑get update and installs zip unzip bc libfreetype6, lists ./bootstrap and ./src, executes bash ./scripts/build.sh, always uploads the deploy and build directories as artifacts, and after the script checks for build/PharoDebug.log to print errors and logs. In the deploy stage add a deploy job that uses the governmentpaas/awscli image, sets GIT_STRATEGY=none, and runs ./deploy/deploy.sh with no branch restrictions.
